content.text: RBAC is in place. Now I'll apply the full CockroachDB deployment — headless service, public service, StatefulSet, and a PodDisruptionBudget to preserve quorum.

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app: crdb-cluster
spec:
  clusterIP: None
  publishNotReadyAddresses: true
  selector:
    app: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-public
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app: crdb-cluster
spec:
  selector:
    app: crdb-cluster
  ports:
  - name: grpc
    port: 26257
    targetPort: 26257
  - name: http
    port: 8080
    targetPort: 8080
---
apiVersion: policy/v1
kind: PodDisruptionBudget
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster-pdb
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  minAvailable: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: crdb-cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        ports:
        - containerPort: 26257
          name: grpc
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -ecx
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-addr=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --logtostderr=WARNING
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF
input.description: Apply headless service, public service, PodDisruptionBudget, and StatefulSet for CockroachDB

content.thinking: The pods are pending and PVCs are pending. This is likely because the PVCs are waiting for storage to be provisioned. Let me wait a bit and check again.

content.text: Pods and PVCs are pending — waiting for storage provisioning. Let me poll until the cluster comes up:

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  echo "=== Check $i ($(date +%H:%M:%S)) ==="
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>&1
  READY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "Running" || true)
  if [ "$READY" -ge 3 ]; then echo "All 3 pods Running"; break; fi
  sleep 10
done

content.thinking:
Only one pod is running and it's not Ready (0/1). The other two pods haven't even been created yet. This is unusual behavior for a StatefulSet - normally StatefulSets create pods sequentially, but pod-1 and pod-2 haven't been created yet.

This is likely because:
1. The StatefulSet is waiting for pod-0 to become Ready before creating pod-1
2. Pod-0 is not Ready because the readiness probe is failing (it requires the cluster to be initialized)

The issue is that CockroachDB needs to be initialized with `cockroach init` before the cluster becomes ready. 

There's a circular dependency here: pod-0 is waiting for the other nodes to join so it can form a quorum and pass the readiness probe, but the StatefulSet won't create those other pods until pod-0 is Ready. I should check the pod logs to see what's actually happening, then figure out how to break this deadlock by initializing the cluster.

content.text: Only `crdb-cluster-0` is up but stuck not-Ready — the StatefulSet's `OrderedReady` policy blocks creating pods 1 and 2 until pod-0 is Ready, but pod-0 needs a quorum to pass the readiness probe. Let me check the log and fix with `Parallel` pod management:

input.description: Check pod-0 logs to confirm it's waiting for peers

content.thinking:
Confirmed: pod-0 is waiting to join pods 1 and 2 which don't exist yet because the StatefulSet is using `OrderedReady` pod management policy. I need to patch the StatefulSet to use `Parallel` pod management policy so all 3 pods start simultaneously.

Let me patch the StatefulSet to use `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`.

content.text: Confirmed — pod-0 is stuck waiting for peers that can't start because of `OrderedReady`. I need to patch the StatefulSet to `Parallel` pod management so all three pods launch simultaneously, then run `cockroach init` to bootstrap the cluster.

input.description: Patch StatefulSet to use Parallel pod management policy

content.thinking:
Kubernetes doesn't allow patching `podManagementPolicy` after creation. I need to delete and recreate the StatefulSet with `Parallel` pod management. I should delete the StatefulSet only (not the PVCs) and recreate it.

Let me delete the StatefulSet and recreate it with `podManagementPolicy: Parallel`.

content.text: `podManagementPolicy` is immutable after creation. I'll delete and recreate the StatefulSet (preserving the existing PVCs) with `Parallel` policy:

input.description: Delete StatefulSet while orphaning pods (keeping PVCs)

pod "crdb-cluster-0" force deleted

tool_use_result.stdout:
Warning: Immediate deletion does not wait for confirmation that the running resource has been terminated. The resource may continue to run on the cluster indefinitely.
pod "crdb-cluster-0" force deleted

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: crdb-cluster
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  serviceName: crdb-cluster
  replicas: 3
  podManagementPolicy: Parallel
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: crdb-cluster
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: crdb-cluster
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: crdb-cluster-sa
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 60
      containers:
      - name: cockroachdb
        image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        ports:
        - containerPort: 26257
          name: grpc
        - containerPort: 8080
          name: http
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -ecx
        - |
          exec /cockroach/cockroach start \
            --insecure \
            --advertise-addr=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local \
            --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 \
            --listen-addr=0.0.0.0:26257 \
            --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257 \
            --logtostderr=WARNING
        env:
        - name: POD_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: metadata.name
        volumeMounts:
        - name: datadir
          mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health?ready=1
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 2
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: datadir
    spec:
      accessModes: [ReadWriteOnce]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 10Gi
EOF
input.description: Recreate StatefulSet with Parallel pod management policy

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 18); do
  echo "=== Check $i ($(date +%H:%M:%S)) ==="
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>&1
  RUNNING=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "Running" || true)
  if [ "$RUNNING" -ge 3 ]; then echo "All 3 pods Running — proceeding to init"; break; fi
  sleep 10
done

  echo "=== Check $i ($(date +%H:%M:%S)) ==="
  kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>&1
  READY=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c "1/1" || true)
  if [ "$READY" -ge 3 ]; then echo "All 3 pods Ready"; break; fi
  sleep 10
done
